basicDetails.schemeName: The Lokpal of India Internship Scheme

schemeSubCategory.value: Education and training grants, fellowship, stipend
schemeSubCategory.label: Education and training grants, fellowship, stipend

schemeContent.detailedDescription_md:
The Lokpal of India launched “The Lokpal of India Internship Scheme”. The interns will get an opportunity to understand anti-corruption mechanisms, including the Lokpal and Lokayuktas Act, 2013, and India’s commitment under the United Nations Convention Against Corruption and related frameworks. They will also learn about the impact of corruption on governance and contribute innovative ideas to strengthen transparency, accountability, and effective functioning of institutions like the Lokpal and other vigilance bodies.
> **Duration:** The period of internship shall be up to 3 months.
> **Attendance:** The interns will have to attend the office on all working days.
schemeContent.benefits_md: - A stipend of ₹10,000/- per month. - The Lokpal of India gives a certificate to the interns completing the internship successfully. - The Lokpal of India provides working space, internet, and other appropriate facilities to the interns.
schemeContent.briefDescription: The Lokpal of India Internship Scheme offers students exposure to anti-corruption laws and institutions. Interns learn about governance, legal frameworks, and contribute ideas to enhance transparency and accountability in public administration.

children.text: The Lokpal of India launched “The Lokpal of India Internship Scheme”. The interns will get an opportunity to understand anti-corruption mechanisms, including the Lokpal and Lokayuktas Act, 2013, and India’s commitment under the United Nations Convention Against Corruption and related frameworks. They will also learn about the impact of corruption on governance and contribute innovative ideas to strengthen transparency, accountability, and effective functioning of institutions like the Lokpal and other vigilance bodies.
Duration:
The period of internship shall be up to 3 months.
Attendance:
The interns will have to attend the office on all working days.
A stipend of ₹10,000/- per month.
The Lokpal of India gives a certificate to the interns completing the internship successfully.
The Lokpal of India provides working space, internet, and other appropriate facilities to the interns.
Step 1:
The interested candidate can apply through their institute/college/university using the
Annexure-I
application form
Step 2:
In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).
Step 3:
Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents by post:
Under Secretary (Establishment), Lokpal of India, 6, Vasant Kunj Institutional Area, Phase-II, New Delhi - 110070.
NOTE:
The final list of selected interns will be displayed on the Lokpal of India website/notice board.

applicationProcess.process_md: **Step 1:** The interested candidate can apply through their institute/college/university using the [Annexure-I](https://lokpal.gov.in/pdfs/Invite_internship.pdf) [application form](https://lokpal.gov.in/pdfs/Advertisement_2023-24_Internship_Scheme_04012024.pdf). **Step 2:** In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required). **Step 3:** Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents by post: > **Under Secretary (Establishment), Lokpal of India, 6, Vasant Kunj Institutional Area, Phase-II, New Delhi - 110070.** **NOTE:** The final list of selected interns will be displayed on the Lokpal of India website/notice board.

eligibilityCriteria.eligibilityDescription_md: 1. The applicant should be a student. 1. The applicant must be enrolled in an institution affiliated with a recognized university within India. 1. The applicant should be pursuing a regular undergraduate or postgraduate degree course in the field of law, political science, public administration, public policy, management, economics, or human rights. 1. The applicant must have secured at least 75% or equivalent marks in the 12th class.1. The undergraduate student must have completed at least two years of study in the undergraduate course. 1. The undergraduate student must have a minimum aggregate of % or equivalent marks in years completed till the date of application. 1. The postgraduate students must have a minimum aggregate of 55% or equivalent in the graduation course. 1. The applicant must have his own laptop for the internship.

children.text: The applicant should be a student.
The applicant must be enrolled in an institution affiliated with a recognized university within India.

The applicant should be pursuing a regular undergraduate or postgraduate degree course in the field of law, political science, public administration, public policy, management, economics, or human rights.
The applicant must have secured at least 75% or equivalent marks in the 12
th
class.
The undergraduate student must have completed at least two years of study in the undergraduate course.
The undergraduate student must have a minimum aggregate of % or equivalent marks in years completed till the date of application.
The postgraduate students must have a minimum aggregate of 55% or equivalent in the graduation course.
The applicant must have his own laptop for the internship.
